name: Magic Link / Passwordless Onboarding
definition_origin: Magic-link auth (email-based passwordless authentication) was popularized by Slack 2014 ('Sign in by Email' link), Medium (Slack-derived 2015), Notion (2016), and standardized in 2019-2024 by auth providers Auth0 (Universal Login passwordless), Clerk (2020+ — 'magic link' as default in B2C SaaS), Stytch (2020), Magic.link (2018), Supabase Auth (2020+ magic-link OTP). The term 'magic link' is industry jargon — sometimes called 'email link auth' or 'OTP link'. Codified across: Auth0 docs (passwordless reference), Stytch onboarding playbook, Clerk B2C SaaS guide, Supabase Auth documentation, Apple Sign in with Apple as adjacent passwordless flow (2019), WebAuthn passkeys spec (FIDO2, 2019; mainstream 2023+). RevenueCat 2025: 35%+ of consumer subscription apps now default to magic-link or social-OAuth; password-default declined sharply 2022-2025. Eliminates the 'Premature Authentication' antipattern (item 51) by reducing friction at signup. Distinct from biometric gate (item 61) which is the REPEAT-SESSION lock; magic link is the FIRST-AUTH method.
core_mechanism: Magic link works by replacing password creation/recall (cognitive load + security risk) with email-link verification — possession-of-email-account replaces knowledge-of-password as the auth factor. Five reinforcing mechanisms: (1) Cognitive load minimization — users no longer need to invent / remember / type a password; just type email and click link. (2) Security uplift — eliminates password reuse / weak passwords / credential-stuffing risk. (3) UX friction shift — moves friction from in-app to email-deep-link round-trip (~30-60 seconds), but the in-app experience feels faster. (4) Account recovery built-in — losing access to email = losing account by design (no separate forgot-password flow). (5) Mobile deep-link integration — tapping email link returns user to app via Universal Links (iOS) / App Links (Android), preserving session state. Trade-off: round-trip latency (30-60s checking email) vs zero-cognitive-effort (no password creation/recall). Best for: B2C consumer apps, AI-wrapper apps, productivity SaaS where users tolerate the round-trip. Worse for: high-frequency-login apps where users want fast password input (banking apps often offer biometric gate as fast-path AFTER initial magic-link auth).
neuroscience_correlate: Working memory load (Sweller cognitive load theory, item 2) is reduced by removing password recall — frees executive function for the actual onboarding goal. Status-quo bias (Samuelson & Zeckhauser 1988) — users defer password creation, abandon onboarding; magic link removes the deferral cause.
empirical_evidence: Slack early data (2014-2015, founder claim): magic-link auth reduced auth-step abandonment by ~50% vs password. Notion (founder podcast 2018): magic-link signup contributes to <90-second onboarding-complete benchmark. Auth0 2023 benchmark study: passwordless auth (magic link + social) shows 22-35% higher signup completion vs password-only. Clerk 2024 data: 60%+ of B2C SaaS using Clerk default to magic-link first; password-only rate dropped to <15%. Stytch 2024 case studies: magic-link adoption correlates with 14-28% lower D1 churn vs password (cohort comparisons). FIDO Alliance 2024: passkey adoption hit 5B accounts (Google + Apple); magic link is the bridge before passkey ubiquity. Cal AI / Prayer Lock / most $30K-$150K/mo solo-AI-wrappers default to magic link or Sign in with Apple. Supabase Auth 2024: 70%+ of Supabase consumer apps use magic link or OAuth; password-only is minority. Email-deliverability research (Postmark, SendGrid 2024): magic-link emails have 95-98% inbox delivery if proper SPF/DKIM/DMARC; abuse-rate <0.5% if rate-limited. OTP fallback (numeric code emailed when link doesn't work — corporate firewalls block links): 5-10% of magic-link users fall back to OTP; OTP completion ~85%.
measurable_outcome_metric: Auth completion rate (% who complete email entry → tap link → return to app — target ≥80%). Time-to-auth (target ≤90 seconds end-to-end). Email-deliverability rate (target ≥98% inbox). OTP fallback rate (% who can't tap link, request OTP — target ≤10%). Post-auth onboarding-resume rate (% who continue past auth — target ≥95%). D1 retention (auth method correlates: magic-link vs password D1 — magic-link should be 14-28% higher per Stytch). Anti-abuse: magic-link request rate per email per hour (rate limit ≤5/hour); OTP attempt count per token (max 5).
conversion_lift_benchmark: Slack early: 50% lower auth abandonment vs password. Auth0 2023: 22-35% higher signup completion. Stytch 2024: 14-28% lower D1 churn vs password. Clerk 2024: 60%+ B2C default to magic link.
engineering_tactics: ARCHITECTURE: (1) USER ENTERS EMAIL on auth screen (single field; no password). (2) BACKEND GENERATES TOKEN (cryptographically random, time-limited, single-use — JWT or opaque random ID stored server-side). (3) EMAIL SENT via transactional ESP (Resend, Postmark, SES, Mailgun) with magic-link URL pointing to deep-link handler + token + 6-digit OTP fallback ('If the link doesn't work, enter code: 123456'). (4) USER TAPS LINK on phone — opens via Universal Links (iOS) / App Links (Android) — verifies token server-side — returns user to in-app onboarding state. (5) DEEP LINK HANDLER: extracts token from URL, posts to backend /verify, receives session JWT, persists to Keychain (iOS) / EncryptedSharedPreferences (Android), navigates to next onboarding step. (6) OTP FALLBACK: alternative path — user types 6-digit code in app instead of tapping link. Necessary for corporate-firewall + email-client-no-link-preview scenarios. (7) RATE-LIMITING (anti-abuse): max 5 magic-link requests per email per hour; max 5 OTP attempts per token before invalidate; CAPTCHA on 3rd request from same IP. (8) TOKEN EXPIRY: 10-15 minutes typical. (9) CROSS-DEVICE flow: user on phone enters email; clicks link from desktop email — must handle gracefully (poll-based session creation, OR just OTP from server requiring user to type code on phone). (10) DEEP-LINK CONFIG: iOS Universal Links via apple-app-site-association file at /.well-known/apple-app-site-association on auth domain. Android App Links via assetlinks.json. App scheme deep-links as fallback (myapp://auth?token=...). (11) BACKEND: Supabase Auth signInWithOtp / signInWithMagicLink (built-in); Clerk Magic Link API; Auth0 Passwordless; Stytch Email Magic Links; or custom (NextAuth.js, Lucia auth). (12) ANTI-ABUSE: monitor email validation (don't send to invalid emails), bot detection (Cloudflare Turnstile or hCaptcha invisible mode on email submit). (13) SESSION DURATION: long-lived refresh token + biometric gate (item 61) for repeat sessions. SHARED STATE: backend stores onboarding_state per user_id; client hydrates on auth-success.
real_world_examples: (1) Slack: 'Sign in by Email' since 2014 — user types email → email arrives with 'Magic link' button → tap → returns to app/web logged in. OTP fallback offered. (2) Notion: magic-link default; OAuth (Google/Apple) secondary. (3) Linear: magic-link or SSO; no password option in default onboarding. (4) Vercel: magic-link or GitHub OAuth. (5) Resend (the dogfood example): magic-link auth on resend.com itself. (6) Cal AI / Prayer Lock / most consumer AI-wrappers: magic-link or Sign in with Apple. (7) Substack: magic-link only — never asks for password. (8) Medium: magic-link default. (9) Stripe: magic-link with OTP fallback. (10) GitHub Mobile (companion app): magic-link to bridge from web session. (11) Supabase dashboard: magic-link or GitHub OAuth. (12) Clerk demo apps: magic-link first, social OAuth second, password as opt-in. (13) Substack: 'No password — we email you a link' explicit framing.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-specific: Universal Links (iOS) + App Links (Android) for deep-link return; complex configuration but seamless UX. Apple iOS Universal Links require apple-app-site-association on the email-link domain (configurable via Web Credential Apple Sign-in too). Android App Links require assetlinks.json on the domain. Without proper config, deep-link opens browser instead of app — broken UX. Web-specific: simpler — link opens browser, browser cookie/localStorage establishes session. Mobile cross-device: user enters email on phone, but checks email on desktop — must show 'continue here' on desktop browser AND poll-back-to-phone-session OR show OTP code on phone screen. Both variants used. WebAuthn passkey auth (item 61 adjacent) increasingly supplements magic link as 2024-2025 standard.
platform_design_language_alignment: Auth screen aligns with Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) — single email field on translucent material works well; primary CTA can use Liquid Glass material. M3 Expressive (Android 16+) — large input + prominent button. Apple Sign in with Apple is the iOS-native passwordless flow; Apple HIG mandates it as primary if any social login offered (Guideline 4.8). Android Credential Manager (2023+) consolidates passwordless flows.
category_fit: Universal — every consumer / B2C / B2B SaaS onboarding can use magic link. Strongest fit: (a) AI-wrapper / chat-first (item 57) — magic link removes friction before the magic moment. (b) productivity / B2B (item 55) — magic link + SSO is the default in 2024-2026 B2B SaaS. (c) wellness / habit (item 54) — magic link or Sign in with Apple. (d) media / content apps. EXCEPTIONS: fintech (item 56) — magic link as ONE auth factor among multiple (must combine with biometric, phone OTP, KYC); high-frequency apps where 30-60s round-trip per login annoys (banking — biometric gate fast-path required). Children's apps under 13 — COPPA requires parental consent; magic link to parent email is one VPC pathway.
solo_dev_feasibility: same_day with managed auth (Supabase Auth, Clerk, Stytch, Magic.link, Auth0 Passwordless). Most consumer auth providers support magic link out-of-box with one API call. 1_week if implementing custom (Lucia, NextAuth.js — DIY token generation + email sending + rate-limiting + deep-link config). Critical infrastructure: Universal Links (iOS) + App Links (Android) configuration is fiddly — allocate 2-3 days for proper setup. Email deliverability: use Resend / Postmark for high inbox rates (free tiers sufficient for early-stage solo apps). Rate-limiting: Supabase Edge Functions or Cloudflare Workers can wrap rate logic.
tooling_options: Auth providers (managed): Supabase Auth (free tier; signInWithOtp), Clerk (B2C-leaning, magic-link first-class), Stytch (developer-focused, magic-link API), Magic.link (specialized in passwordless, also Web3), Auth0 Universal Login (Passwordless connection), Firebase Auth (signInWithEmailLink). Self-host: NextAuth.js (Email provider), Lucia auth, custom Express + JWT. Email ESPs (transactional): Resend (best DX, free tier), Postmark (high-deliverability), SendGrid, AWS SES (cheapest at scale), Mailgun. Deep-link: Universal Links / App Links manual config; Branch.io / Adjust deep-link SDKs (paid). Rate-limiting: Upstash Ratelimit, Cloudflare Turnstile, Supabase Auth built-in rate-limiting. WebAuthn passkey libraries (adjacent): SimpleWebAuthn, Stytch Passkeys, Clerk Passkeys, Apple Sign in with Apple framework.
ai_dependency: none for the auth flow itself. Optional: AI-detection of suspicious magic-link request patterns (anomaly-detection — small ML model). Not load-bearing.
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment: 'Build magic-link auth as the default onboarding auth method. (1) AUTH SCREEN: single email field, primary CTA "Send magic link" (or "Continue with email"). Secondary buttons: Sign in with Apple (top — Apple HIG requires if other social offered), Sign in with Google. NO password field by default. (2) BACKEND: use Supabase Auth signInWithOtp({ email, options: { emailRedirectTo: "https://yourapp.com/auth/confirm", shouldCreateUser: true }}) — this generates token + sends email + handles redirect. Or Clerk Magic Link API. Or Stytch. Or custom (Resend + crypto.randomUUID + Supabase row). (3) EMAIL TEMPLATE: from-name brand, subject "Sign in to [App]", body single primary button "Sign in to [App]" + 6-digit OTP fallback ("If the link doesn''t work, enter this code: 123456"). Plain-text alt-version. SPF/DKIM/DMARC on sending domain. (4) DEEP LINK HANDLER: handle https://yourapp.com/auth/confirm?token=... via Universal Links (iOS) / App Links (Android). On verify, persist session to Keychain/EncryptedSharedPreferences. (5) OTP FALLBACK: in-app screen "Or enter the 6-digit code from your email" — single 6-digit input. (6) RATE-LIMITING: Supabase Auth built-in or custom — max 5 magic-link requests per email per hour, max 5 OTP attempts per token. (7) ANTI-ABUSE: Cloudflare Turnstile invisible mode on email submit. (8) DEEP-LINK CONFIG: apple-app-site-association at /.well-known/ on auth domain (iOS Universal Links); assetlinks.json (Android App Links). (9) SESSION: long-lived refresh token + biometric gate (item 61) for repeat sessions. Forbid: password as default, password as required, missing OTP fallback, broken deep-link config (opens browser instead of app).' Second fragment: 'Build the auth-confirm deep-link landing screen — shows brief loading spinner while token verifies, then transitions seamlessly to next onboarding step. NO marketing screen, NO "Welcome back!" interstitial — this is the one-time auth confirmation, not a moment to interrupt.'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup the auth screen — minimal, restrained. Brand mark top (32px height, monochrome, NOT colored gradient). Headline 32pt Cabinet Grotesk weight 400 "Sign in to [App]". Subhead 16pt Söhne neutral-grey "We''ll email you a magic link". Single email field — 56pt height, 1px border ink/15, 16pt Söhne placeholder "Email". Primary CTA "Send magic link" 56pt height, solid ember-orange #E36B2C (NOT gradient), ink-color text. Below: "or" separator (8pt small-caps Söhne Mono, hairline). "Sign in with Apple" black native button (top — Apple HIG mandate). "Continue with Google" white-on-white-bordered native button. Background: solid bone #F4EFE6 (NOT gradient, NOT 3D illustration). Forbid: Inter font (use Söhne/Cabinet Grotesk), indigo-500 button (use ember-orange), purple-blue gradient background, ✨🚀 emoji decorations, 3-column "Why magic link" feature explainer, hover:scale-105 on button, rounded-xl on input field (use 8px). Aesthetic feels like Linear/Notion — restrained, opinionated, confident. Second screen: "Check your email" — large icon (custom illustration of envelope, NOT Lucide Mail), headline 28pt "We sent a link to [email]", subhead 14pt "Tap the link in your email to sign in". Below: "Didn''t get it? Resend in 60s" countdown text-link, AND "Or enter 6-digit code" expandable to OTP input. Forbid: same anti-slop bans.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI typically (1) defaults to password-required auth (over-corrects from older training data), (2) skips OTP fallback (user can't auth in firewall-blocked corporate email), (3) skips Universal Links / App Links config (link opens browser instead of app), (4) defaults to indigo-500 + purple-blue gradient on auth screen (slop), (5) puts Sign in with Apple BELOW Google/Email instead of top (Apple HIG violation 4.8), (6) skips rate-limiting (vulnerable to magic-link spam), (7) writes overly-cute 'Magic ✨ link' button copy, (8) omits proper SPF/DKIM/DMARC on email-sending domain (deliverability tanks), (9) uses wrong token expiry (too long = security risk; too short = users miss window), (10) skips persistence to Keychain (session lost on app restart). Counter-prompt: 'Build magic-link auth with EXPLICIT REQUIREMENTS: (1) Email-only field, NO password by default. (2) Sign in with Apple TOP if any social offered (Apple HIG 4.8). (3) OTP fallback (6-digit code) for firewall scenarios. (4) Universal Links + App Links properly configured (apple-app-site-association + assetlinks.json). (5) Rate-limiting 5/hour per email + bot detection. (6) Token expiry 10-15 min, single-use. (7) Session persisted to Keychain/EncryptedSharedPreferences. (8) Forbid: password as default, indigo-500/purple-blue gradient on auth, ✨ emoji on "magic link" button, Inter font, hover:scale-105.'
anti_patterns: (1) Password-required default (out of doctrine 2024-2026; use magic link or social OAuth). (2) Missing OTP fallback (corporate-firewall users can't auth). (3) Missing Universal Links / App Links config (link opens browser, breaks UX). (4) No rate-limiting (magic-link spam abuse). (5) 'Magic ✨ Link' cutesy button copy (slop signal). (6) Sign in with Apple below other social (Apple HIG 4.8 violation). (7) Token expiry too long (security risk) or too short (UX friction — users miss window). (8) Email from generic 'noreply@' (deliverability tanks; use brand-name@ or auth@). (9) Skipping email DMARC config (spam-folder relegation). (10) Cross-device flow broken (user on phone, email on desktop — no resolution path). (11) Indigo-500 + gradient + Inter slop default on auth screen. (12) Session not persisted across app restart (forces re-auth — cardinal sin).
regulatory_risk: Low-Medium. (a) GDPR — email collection requires lawful basis (legitimate interest for auth typically suffices). (b) CCPA — same. (c) ePrivacy Directive — auth emails are transactional, exempt from marketing-consent. (d) FTC unfair-marketing — auth emails must not include marketing without consent. (e) NIST 800-63B — magic link is acceptable single-factor auth at AAL1; AAL2 requires possession + knowledge or possession + inherence (combine with biometric for higher assurance). (f) PCI DSS — fintech apps require multi-factor; magic link alone insufficient. (g) HIPAA — magic-link OK for auth but session must be MFA for PHI access. (h) GDPR right to erasure — must delete user account + associated magic-link tokens.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store Guideline 4.8 — Sign in with Apple required if Google/Facebook/Microsoft auth offered. Apple Guideline 5.1.1 — privacy policy mandatory. Apple Universal Links require apple-app-site-association on TLS-secured domain. iOS 16+ ASAuthorization for Apple ID auth. Android 13+ Credential Manager API for unified passwordless. Android App Links require assetlinks.json verified. Email — CAN-SPAM requires unsubscribe + sender ID; transactional auth emails generally exempt but include footer. EU GDPR + ePrivacy compliance. Google's email auth requires SPF/DKIM/DMARC for inbox delivery (post-Feb 2024 Gmail bulk-sender rules).
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 — auth screen must be screen-reader accessible. Email field must have proper label (SC 1.3.1, SC 4.1.2) — autofocus with screen-reader announcement. Touch target 24×24 px (SC 2.5.8) for primary button. Color contrast 4.5:1 (SC 1.4.3) on email field and primary button. Sufficient time (SC 2.2.1) — magic-link expiry should be ≥10 minutes; OTP entry should not auto-submit. Redundant entry (SC 3.3.7) — don't ask user to re-type email if they go back. Magic-link email itself must be accessible (proper HTML email, alt text on button image, plain-text alternative). EU EAA 2025 enforcement.
pitfalls_caveats: (1) Email deliverability is fragile — Gmail Feb 2024 bulk-sender rules require SPF + DKIM + DMARC + low spam complaint rate; misconfigured domains land in spam = magic-link broken. (2) Universal Links / App Links config — incorrect setup = link opens browser instead of app (cardinal UX failure); test on physical devices. (3) Cross-device flow is the most common bug — user enters email on phone, opens email on desktop, tap link → desktop browser (not phone app). Solutions: poll on phone for session creation, OR show OTP for cross-device. (4) Corporate firewalls strip links from emails — OTP fallback essential. (5) Token expiry trade-off — 5min (good security, miss window UX) vs 30min (user-friendly, security risk). 10-15min standard. (6) Session persistence across app restart — must use Keychain (iOS) / EncryptedSharedPreferences (Android), not UserDefaults / SharedPreferences (insecure). (7) Email subject + sender — must clearly identify as auth (not marketing); use brand name in From. (8) Anti-abuse: bot signups; rate-limit + Cloudflare Turnstile invisible mode. (9) iOS Mail Privacy Protection (2021+) preloads images — affects email tracking but not deliverability. (10) Email-deliverability monitoring (Postmark, Mailgun deliverability dashboards) — detect issues before users report.
cross_cultural_validity: JP: corporate email is dominant; magic-link works but business-formal sender register required. KR: Naver Mail, Daum dominant — deliverability requires Korea-specific SPF/DKIM. CN: foreign email providers blocked — must use China-resident email or fall back to phone OTP for CN users. EU: GDPR-strict; data residency for EU users (use EU-region email infra — Postmark EU, Resend EU). LATAM: WhatsApp dominant — magic-link works but WhatsApp OTP increasingly preferred. India: phone OTP via SMS more common than email magic-link (Aadhaar-linked auth, UPI). MENA: phone OTP / WhatsApp OTP common. RTL Arabic: email RTL rendering required; subject line + body RTL.
prescribed_action: DEFAULT auth method for B2C consumer apps in 2024-2026. SEQUENCE: (1) Auth screen: single email field, NO password. Sign in with Apple TOP if other social offered (Apple HIG 4.8). Sign in with Google secondary. (2) On submit: backend generates token, sends email via transactional ESP (Resend/Postmark) with magic-link button + 6-digit OTP fallback. SPF/DKIM/DMARC mandatory. (3) Universal Links (iOS) + App Links (Android) properly configured for deep-link return. (4) Token expiry 10-15 min, single-use. (5) Rate-limit 5/hour per email; CAPTCHA on 3rd request. (6) OTP fallback for firewall scenarios. (7) Session persisted to Keychain/EncryptedSharedPreferences. (8) Pair with biometric gate (item 61) for repeat sessions. Use managed auth (Supabase Auth, Clerk, Stytch) for solo-dev — same-day implementation. Forbid: password as default, indigo-500/purple-blue gradient on auth screen, ✨ emoji on 'magic link' copy, missing OTP fallback, missing rate-limiting, broken deep-link config.
questionnaire_relevance: N/A — auth is upstream of questionnaire. Magic link enables removing 'Premature Authentication' antipattern by being faster than password creation, allowing onboarding to flow seamlessly past auth.
aha_relevance: Auth itself is NOT the aha — but magic-link removes friction before the aha. The 30-60s email round-trip can break the onboarding flow if poorly implemented; well-implemented (deep-link returns user to exactly where they left off) preserves the aha trajectory.
telemetry_events: auth_screen_viewed, auth_method_selected (email/apple/google), magic_link_email_submitted, magic_link_email_sent, magic_link_email_delivered (via webhook from ESP), magic_link_clicked, magic_link_token_verified, magic_link_session_created, otp_fallback_requested, otp_submitted, otp_verified, auth_completed, auth_abandoned, auth_rate_limited, anti_abuse_captcha_triggered. Cohort: D1 retention by auth_method.
measurement_horizon: Day 0 (auth completion); Day 1 (return-rate by auth method).
emotional_archetype_alignment: Magic-link auth itself is archetype-neutral — works for all archetypes when copy is matched. Caregiver: 'We''ll email you a sign-in link — no password to remember'. Sage: 'Sign in with email — passwordless and secure'. Magician: 'A link arrives in your inbox — tap to sign in'. Avoid 'Magic ✨ Link' Jester register (cutesy slop). Avoid Outlaw register on auth (rebellion clashes with security-context).
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Premature Authentication (item 51) — magic link reduces auth friction so signup-before-value-demo is less harmful. Pairs against Password Default (cardinal antipattern in 2024-2026 B2C). Pairs against Broken Deep-Link Config, Missing OTP Fallback, No Rate-Limiting, Indigo-500-Slop-Auth-Screen.
